items.title: Pope Leo XIV in Lebanon: What political message can we expect him to deliver?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In Lebanon, Pope Leo has now begun the second leg of first overseas visit as pontiff.

The pope is expected to appeal for peace in the region as the focus of his visit. Lebanon is still being hit by Israeli airstrikes, despite a ceasefire. Lebanon is a  Muslim-majority country, but around a third of the population are Christians.  Crowds gathered to welcome the Pope hours before his plane touched down.

00:00 Pope Leo XIV arrives in Beirut
00:39 Stella Männer, DW Correspondent

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Venezuela, Russia's ‘shadow fleet’ and AI shopping
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Donald Trump says Venezuelan airspace should be considered closed as tensions mount with Nicolas Maduros’ government. Ukraine says naval drones hit two tankers from Russia’s ‘shadow fleet’. Hondurans vote in a divisive election. Plus, we look at how retailers are trying to get on your AI holiday shopping list.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV wraps up Turkey visit | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo is concluding his trip to Turkey, where he's offered another olive branch across a sectarian - and for his hosts, political frontier. After earlier meetings with Muslim clerics and the patriarch of Orthodox Christianity, the American-born pontiff visited the Armenian Apostolic Cathedral in Istanbul. With some 50,000 members, it's the largest Christian community in Turkey. But it's also a source of political friction. Many countries including Germany call the World War One-era mass killings of Armenians by Ottoman Turks a 'genocide.'

00:00 Pope Leo XIV wraps up Turkey visit
01:22 Dorian Jones, DW Correspondent

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

The victims included both children and adults, said Heather Brent, a spokesperson for the San Joaquin County sheriff’s office. Early indications “suggest this may have been a targeted incident,” Brent said during a news conference at the scene.

items.title: Africa's Investment Landscape: Highlights From the Bloomberg Africa Business Summit
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>At the Bloomberg Africa Business Summit, influential leaders, investors, and innovators gathered to discuss the forces reshaping the continent’s economic future. From policy shifts and technology breakthroughs to sustainability, finance and trade partnerships, Jennifer Zabasajja brings you key insights and exclusive conversations with the people driving Africa’s next era of growth, including African Rainbow Minerals' Patrice Motsepe, Rand Merchant Bank's Emrie Brown, Egypt's Hassan El Khatib, Standard Chartered's Benjamin Hung and South Africa's Ronald Lamola.
--------

items.title: Pope Leo XIV visits Blue Mosque in attempt to promote interfaith dialogue | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo has joined other religious leaders in Turkey with a message of reconciliation on his first trip abroad as pontiff. The pope took part in a service with Patiarch Bartholomew, the head of the Orthodox Church. The two signed a joint declaration to heal a nearly 1,000-year rift between their churches. The pontiff also joined Muslim leaders on a tour of Istanbul's Blue Mosque. And Pope Leo later held a mass attended by tens of thousands in an arena in Istanbul. 

Chapters:
0:00 Pope Leo tours Blue Mosque
0:33 Meghan J. Clark, St. John's University

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Large parts of Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand have been 
stricken by cyclone-fuelled torrential rain for a week, with a 
rare tropical storm forming in the Malacca Strait. (Reuters)
The catastrophe is one of the deadliest natural disasters to hit 
Indonesia this year, underscoring the country's escalating 
vulnerability to climate-driven weather extremes.

items.title: Thousands protest in Germany against AfD youth wing's founding meeting | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Here in Germany, thousands of demonstrators have gathered in the western city of Giessen to protest against the founding meeting of the far-right Alternative for Germany's new youth wing. 6-thousand police are standing by. Generation Deutschland, as the new group is expected to be called, replaces the AfD's previous association, which was confirmed by the courts as right wing extremist. The changes aim to allow the AfD to exert more control over it's youth movement.
